characters.name: Consultant Son
characters.publicRole: Arrogant, hardworking son running the family business.
characters.personality: Consultant‑core; no friends; poor social skills; sees himself as the obvious successor.
characters.secretPressure: The company is going public and he will not inherit operational control.
characters.wants: Control of Baroo Holdings and recognition as successor.
characters.fears: Being reduced to a mere board seat while the Director controls the transition.
characters.redHerring: Loudest apparent motive makes him an early suspect.

actObjectives.ACT1: Assert his authority at dinner and ensure the reading proceeds on his terms.
actObjectives.ACT2: Lead the search with an air of command while privately doubting others.
actObjectives.ACT3: Defend his entitlement when the codicil threatens his control and deflect suspicion.

clues: Succession briefing in the media room indicating his future role.
Director’s hostile email showing contempt for his consultancy background.

characters.name: International Daughter
characters.publicRole: Boarding‑school graduate and golden‑spoon daughter from France.
characters.personality: Self‑sufficient and observant despite her pampered image.
characters.secretPressure: Uncertain why J. Baroo trusted her and wary of being dragged into company politics.
characters.wants: Truth about her father’s trust and a fair say in the estate.
characters.redHerring: Appears frivolous but may expose the Director.

actObjectives.ACT1: Observe the dinner, test her brother’s arrogance, and quietly collect information.
actObjectives.ACT2: Search thoroughly and question why the fake envelope exists.
actObjectives.ACT3: Challenge the Director and push for a just reading of the codicil.

clues: Archive transfer letter in the media room hinting at her father’s trust.
Note about the Baroo archive revealing hidden assets.

characters.name: Widow
characters.personality: Dignified on the surface but estranged from J. Baroo.
characters.secretPressure: Using the Bastard Child claim as leverage to secure status and settlement.
characters.wants: Legal recognition of her status, residence rights in Baroo Manor, and a generous settlement.
characters.fears: Revelation of her estranged marriage and loss of standing.
characters.redHerring: Anger at the Publicist and rivalry with the Opera Singer create suspicion.

actObjectives.ACT1: Display grief while subtly advancing her leverage through the Bastard claim.
actObjectives.ACT2: Watch for opportunities to align with those who support her settlement.
actObjectives.ACT3: Argue for her entitlement and conceal the true state of her marriage.

clues: Estrangement papers in the media room detailing marital issues.
Statements aligning her with the Bastard Child.

characters.name: Publicist / Family Brand Manager
characters.publicRole: Controller of the Baroo family image and public statements.
characters.personality: Polished and calculating, always thinking in terms of narrative and optics.
characters.secretPressure: Letters, scandals, illegitimate child rumours, Opera Singer revelations, and a manufactured public image on the verge of collapse.
characters.wants: Tight control of the family narrative and no damaging leaks.
characters.fears: Exposure of crises that would ruin the family brand.
characters.redHerring: The pre‑written crisis statement suggests premeditated spin.

actObjectives.ACT1: Manage perceptions during dinner and deflect any questions about scandals.
actObjectives.ACT2: Ensure the fake envelope narrative supports the brand and spin contradictions.
actObjectives.ACT3: Craft statements that minimise reputational damage once the truth emerges.

clues: Crisis statement in the media room addressing potential scandals.
Notes on illegitimate child rumours and Opera Singer correspondence.

characters.name: Unwanted Bastard Child
characters.publicRole: Possible illegitimate child of J. Baroo seeking recognition.
characters.personality: Defiant, determined to be acknowledged and compensated.
characters.secretPressure: Possesses paternity evidence and knows the Widow may be using them as a weapon.
characters.wants: Recognition and a share of the estate commensurate with their bloodline.
characters.fears: Being dismissed as a fraud and losing all influence.
characters.redHerring: May appear opportunistic but genuinely holds evidence.

actObjectives.ACT1: Present their claim and gauge who may support or oppose them.
actObjectives.ACT2: Search for further evidence or allies.
actObjectives.ACT3: Push for formal acknowledgement when the codicil is read.

clues: Paternity file in the media room suggesting their lineage.
Letters hinting at secret meetings between J. Baroo and the Opera Singer.

characters.name: Director / Best Friend
characters.publicRole: J. Baroo’s long‑time friend and senior director of Baroo Holdings, appearing calm and kind to the Son.
characters.personality: Calculating and ambitious; hides resentment behind a mentor facade.
characters.secretPressure: Privately despises the Son and wishes to seize control of the company. Drafts a fake codicil to achieve this.
characters.wants: Absolute control of Baroo Holdings and a guaranteed role as Controller of the Board.
characters.fears: Being excluded from control if the real codicil is read.
characters.redHerring: Acts supportive until Act 2, making his motive harder to detect early on.

actObjectives.ACT1: Play the mentor while secretly plotting the blackout and envelope swap.
actObjectives.ACT2: Misdirect search efforts towards the fake envelope and keep the real one hidden.
actObjectives.ACT3: Defend the fake codicil as genuine and deflect accusations until exposed.

clues: Aggressive email in the media room revealing disdain for the Son.
Hidden real envelope in The Blind Judge statue.

characters.name: Philanthropist
characters.personality: Smooth and morally persuasive, uses charitable language to mask financial interests.
characters.secretPressure: Worries about an audit or freeze of the foundation’s funds.
characters.wants: Pledges honoured and no scrutiny of the foundation’s accounts.
characters.fears: Suspension of the foundation or exposure of power‑broking.
characters.redHerring: Moral language hides genuine influence and money.

actObjectives.ACT1: Lobby for continued support to the foundation and downplay any audit concerns.
actObjectives.ACT2: Search the grounds while ensuring the foundation’s image remains spotless.
actObjectives.ACT3: Argue against freezing funds when the real codicil is read.

clues: Foundation audit document in the media room suggesting irregularities.

characters.name: Minister of State Planning
characters.publicRole: Government official responsible for planning and infrastructure.
characters.personality: Reserved and strategic; attends under the pretext of paying respects but has vested interests.
characters.secretPressure: Land and planning arrangements with Baroo Holdings are at risk if exposed.
characters.wants: Continuation of development deals and protection of public‑private partnerships.
characters.fears: Suspension of the deals or public scandal over collusion.
characters.redHerring: Presence at the will reading seems out of place and raises questions.

actObjectives.ACT1: Maintain a low profile and avoid drawing attention to government ties.
actObjectives.ACT2: Gather intelligence on what others know about planning deals.
actObjectives.ACT3: Protect official interests when the codicil threatens to suspend arrangements.

clues: Planning file in the media room indicating questionable arrangements.

characters.name: Antique Hunter
characters.publicRole: Eccentric collector and advisor on the Baroo Collection.
characters.personality: Old‑school tomb raider type, charming yet secretive.
characters.secretPressure: Fears a provenance review will expose stolen or fake artefacts.
characters.wants: No review of the Baroo Collection and continued access to rare objects.
characters.fears: Exposure of questionable provenance and loss of advisory role.
characters.redHerring: Known for carrying tools like a crowbar, making them a convenient suspect.

actObjectives.ACT1: Downplay the importance of the collection and keep the focus off the archives.
actObjectives.ACT2: Search the grounds with an eye for hidden objects and defend possession of tools.
actObjectives.ACT3: Argue against any provenance review when the codicil is read.

clues: Provenance file in the media room detailing disputed artefacts.
Crowbar used in the attempted escape.

characters.name: Famous Opera Singer
characters.publicRole: Glamorous artist and beneficiary of Baroo patronage.
characters.personality: Charismatic and dramatic but protective of private correspondence.
characters.secretPressure: Fears personal letters and the truth about the Bastard Child’s parentage will become public.
characters.wants: A private bequest and discretion around personal affairs.
characters.fears: Exposure of correspondence that could damage reputation or implicate her.
characters.redHerring: Her rivalry with the Widow for J. Baroo’s affection gives her an apparent motive.

actObjectives.ACT1: Perform grief convincingly while keeping personal affairs hidden.
actObjectives.ACT2: Look for letters or evidence that others may exploit.
actObjectives.ACT3: Secure her bequest and protect her reputation when the codicil is read.

clues: Opera file in the media room containing correspondence.
Letters hinting at the Bastard Child’s parentage.
